name: First State Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering checking, savings, business loans, and agriculture financing with online and mobile banking access.
description_long:
First State Bank is a traditional community bank headquartered in Belmond, Iowa, operating as an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government. The bank has been serving its local community with deposit and lending products, as evidenced by their routing number 073921543 and established web presence.

The bank offers a range of financial products including personal savings accounts, checking accounts (Value Checking), online banking with username-based access, and mobile banking through a downloadable application. On the lending side, First State Bank provides business loans for inventory and building purchases, agriculture loans tailored to farming operations, and personal savings account options. Their digital channels include online banking enrollment and password recovery tools, plus a mobile app for account access on-the-go.

First State Bank distinguishes itself through specialized agriculture lending, reflecting its Iowa location and rural customer base. The bank emphasizes accessibility through both digital channels (online and mobile banking) and traditional banking services. Their FDIC insurance status and community focus position them as a stable, regulated financial institution rather than a fintech alternative.

As a traditional community bank, First State Bank serves customers seeking established banking infrastructure with local ties. However, the website provides minimal detail about rates, fees, loan terms, or competitive advantages. Prospective customers would need to contact the bank directly for specific product details, terms, and competitive comparisons with larger regional or national institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking platform with username enrollment
Business loans for inventory and building
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government guarantee
Specialized agriculture financing tailored to farming operations
Mobile banking app for account access on-the-go
Online banking with account enrollment available
Value Checking account option for personal use
Business loans available for inventory and building purchases
Established community bank with routing number on file
cons: Website provides no information on interest rates, APYs, or loan terms
No details on fees, minimum balances, or account requirements
Limited geographic reach as a small community bank may restrict access
No information about customer service hours or phone support
Minimal product comparison data to evaluate competitiveness
best_for: Iowa residents and rural customers needing agriculture financing
Small business owners in the Belmond area seeking local lending relationships
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and traditional banking security
Agricultural operators requiring specialized farm financing
diagnosis: First State Bank is best for customers in rural Iowa seeking traditional community banking with FDIC protection and agricultural financing options. The primary caveat is the lack of published rate, fee, and term information online, requiring direct contact with the bank for pricing and competitive comparison.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
